text: <par>This Qiang Mawo form bears a superficial resemblance to Chepang and Magar <latinform>sās</latinform>, although the latter two are undoubtedly borrowings from IA (cf. Sanskrit <latinform>śvas-</latinform>). The initial velar spirants in the Qiang Mawo and Ergong forms seem cognate to the prefixal <latinform>g-</latinform> in WT.</par>

<par>Hayu shows variation between homorganic final nasal and stop, with the allofam in <latinform>-t</latinform> perhaps representing the same suffixal element as Qiang Mawo <latinform>-s</latinform>.</par>
<par>M1: p or 1013?? M2: 1388? See records below.</par>
<par>2nd morpheme here is a verb on uncertain meaning used together with /<latinform>qʰʂə/</latinform> ‘shit, fart’  to mean ‘break wind’ </par>
